name: Pago Pago, American Samoa

visa.notes: US nationals (American Samoans) and US citizens — no visa required. American Samoa is an unincorporated US territory.

rent.notes: Limited formal rental market; many live in family/village housing.

groceries.notes: Cost-U-Less, KS Mart; nearly all dry goods imported.

transportation.notes: Aiga bus system cheap; most expats keep a vehicle.

phoneCell.notes: ASTCA, BlueSky; data costs higher than mainland.

taxes.notes: Estimated monthly income tax on $72,000/yr household income. Based on jurisdiction's effective retiree rate; excludes sales/VAT/property tax.

medicine.notes: LBJ Tropical Medical Center pharmacy; chronic meds often mail-order from Hawaii/mainland.

medicalOOP.notes: Specialist care routinely requires travel to Honolulu (5.5h flight).

healthcarePreMedicare.notes: ACA silver benchmark for 2-adult household both age ~60, before subsidies. County-level estimate (American Samoa, AS). LBJ Tropical Medical Center. Very limited private coverage. Estimated ±15% vs healthcare.gov quote — verify for a specific ZIP before relying on the number.

healthcare.system: LBJ Tropical Medical Center (public, sole hospital); Medicaid/Medicare partially applicable
healthcare.waitTimes: Long; medevac to Honolulu common for complex cases

acaMarketplace.notes: LBJ Tropical Medical Center. Very limited private coverage.
acaMarketplace.disclaimer: Estimated ±15% vs healthcare.gov quote — verify for a specific ZIP before relying on the number.

pros: US territory — USD, US legal framework

cons.text: Very remote — few flights, long travel to mainland
High import costs on most goods

taxes.notes: American Samoa has its own tax code (mirrored with modifications); residents file with ASG Tax Office, not IRS.
